evals.prompt: I need help planning an SPFx web part for SharePoint Online with React and TypeScript. It needs custom property pane controls, propertiesMetadata for search-friendly fields, tenant deployment, theme awareness, and Microsoft Graph calls. Walk me through the architecture decisions first.
evals.expected_output: Routes through web parts, UI, data-client, and deployment workstreams with SharePoint Online-first guidance.

evals.prompt: We have a classic script injection and a UserCustomAction from an older SharePoint solution. I want to move to a supported modern client-side approach, but I am not sure whether this becomes an Application Customizer, Command Set, Field Customizer, or Form Customizer.
evals.expected_output: Uses migration and extension selection guidance instead of classic farm-solution advice.

evals.prompt: Review this SPFx project for SharePoint Server 2019 or Subscription Edition support. I need the right Node, React, TypeScript, generator, and build-tool expectations, and I want any ambiguity called out clearly instead of guessed away.
evals.expected_output: Uses conservative compatibility guidance, explicitly names the Subscription Edition doc ambiguity, and stays in the SPFx lane.

evals.prompt: We want to expose an SPFx web part in Teams and also wire a Viva Connections dashboard experience into the same solution. What supportedHosts, deployment, packaging, and host-boundary decisions should we make first?
evals.expected_output: Routes through Teams and Viva plus deployment guidance without pretending SPFx owns non-UI Teams capabilities.

evals.prompt: Should this SPFx solution call SharePoint with SPHttpClient, add PnPjs, use MSGraphClientV3, or use AadHttpClient for a custom API? We also need package-solution permission requests handled safely.
evals.expected_output: Chooses the correct client per boundary, explains permission-request behavior, and separates runtime API access from provisioning tasks.
